narration: What if I told you a simple sugar can transform into alcohol?

narration: This is called alcoholic fermentation, a process used for centuries in brewing and baking.

narration: It all starts with yeast. This tiny fungus munches on sugar, producing alcohol and carbon dioxide.
visual_description: Escalation visual: It all starts with yeast. This tiny fungus munches on sugar, producing alcohol and carbon dioxide.

narration: But not all yeast acts the same. Different strains yield different flavors and alcohol levels.
